findings.summary: Authored PROP-050 with concrete patches for both A (Git Data API fallback in build.js + decider self-apply + new monitor/scripts/push-via-api.js) and B (workspace-sync regen docs/index.html on FUSE mtime drift). 6 patches total across 4 files + 1 new file. All six counter-arguments rebutted. PDF regeneration explicitly punted per operator preference.
findings.action: Awaiting operator + cowork-claude review and application. No tinker self-apply per directive scope_authority.

findings.summary: Confirmed decider and workspace-sync use the SAME PAT (both extract from workspace git remote URL). Asymmetry in push success rate is per-session/IP, not per-PAT. Design A works by bypassing git's HTTPS push code path entirely (REST API). Design B works because workspace-sync's session/IP has empirically not been on the 403 list.

evidence.decider_pat_source: workspace remote URL (inherited by decider clone)
evidence.success_rate_quote: 32+ push-failures over 5 days, 100% workspace-sync push success (per workspace-sync.md operational notes L506)
